text: William James wrote in 1890 that 'the faculty of voluntarily bringing back a wandering attention, over and over again, is the very root of judgment, character, and will.' He was describing a capacity that neuroscience would later locate in the prefrontal cortex — specifically in the anterior cingulate, which monitors for attentional lapses and issues corrective signals. What James could not foresee was that this corrective mechanism depends on a relatively low-cortisol, low-norepinephrine neurochemical environment to operate. The wandering attention he urged us to reclaim is not a moral failure; it is, in part, the brain faithfully reporting the state of its own stress chemistry.
question: Where did neuroscience later locate the capacity William James described as 'voluntarily bringing back a wandering attention'?

options.text: The hippocampus, which stores attentional templates
The cerebellum, which coordinates cognitive timing
The prefrontal cortex, specifically the anterior cingulate
The locus coeruleus, which broadcasts alerting signals
